name: Ride to Trasierra - circular from La Parra
name_original: Rodeo a Trasierra – circular desde La Parra
region_intro: Hiking around La Parra, located in the province of Badajoz, Extremadura, Spain, offers diverse terrain for outdoor exploration. The region features a limestone mountain range, providing a scenic backdrop for many trails. The landscape includes varied terrain suitable for different fitness levels, with routes traversing both open areas and paths leading to natural features. Best hiking trails around La Parra The most popular hiking route is Limestone Mountain Range – circular from La Parra (SL-BA 194), a 5.1 miles (8.2 km) trail that takes 2 hours 24 minutes to complete. This moderate route features a limestone mountain range. Another top favourite among local hikers is Pico Mirrio Trail, a moderate 4.9 miles (7.8 km) path. This route features significant elevation gain, reaching 308 meters. Local hikers also love the Castillo de Nogales — circular by Los Ejidos, a 2.9 miles (4.7 km) trail leading through the area around Castillo de Nogales, often completed in about 1 hour 20 minutes. Hiking around La Parra is defined by its limestone mountain range, varied terrain, and paths leading to historic sites. The network offers options for different ability levels, from shorter paths to more challenging routes. The routes in La Parra are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 30 reviews. More than 40 hikers have used komoot to explore La Parra's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around La Parra?
A: There are over 20 hiking routes around La Parra, offering a variety of experiences for different skill levels. You'll find paths traversing the limestone mountain range and varied terrain.

Q: What are the typical difficulty levels for hikes in La Parra?
A: The trails around La Parra cater to various fitness levels. You can find easy paths suitable for beginners, but the majority of routes are moderate, requiring good fitness. There are also a few more challenging options for experienced hikers.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in La Parra?
A: Yes, La Parra offers several circular routes. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e991603066/limestone-mountain-range-circular-from-la-parra-sl-ba-194" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Limestone Mountain Range – circular from La Parra (SL-BA 194)&lt;/a&gt; is a popular moderate option, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e991605926/ride-to-trasierra-circular-from-la-parra" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ride to Trasierra - circular from La Parra&lt;/a&gt; also offers a scenic loop.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on the hiking trails?
A: The region is characterized by a diverse landscape. You'll encounter trails through a scenic limestone mountain range, as well as varied terrain that includes both open areas and paths leading to natural features. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e991291225/pico-mirrio-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico Mirrio Trail&lt;/a&gt;, for instance, features significant elevation gain, showcasing the area's hilly nature.

Q: Are there any historical landmarks or points of interest to see while hiking?
A: Absolutely. The area around La Parra is rich in history and natural beauty. You can explore sites like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6249042" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Nogales Castle&lt;/a&gt;, or visit the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1124589" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Juan Hermitage of La Parra&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e991621788/castillo-de-nogales-circular-by-los-ejidos" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de Nogales — circular by Los Ejidos&lt;/a&gt; route specifically takes you through the area around Nogales Castle.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in La Parra?
A: The hiking trails in La Parra are highly regarded by the komoot community, with an average rating of 4.6 stars from over 30 reviews. Hikers often praise the diverse terrain and the opportunities to explore both natural landscapes and historic sites.

Q: Are there longer hiking options for more experienced hikers?
A: Yes, for those looking for a longer challenge, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1222605650/nogales-castle-and-reservoir-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Nogales Castle and Reservoir Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a substantial 14.8 km (9.2 miles) route that can take around 4 hours to complete, offering a comprehensive exploration of the area.

Q: Are the trails around La Parra suitable for families with children?
A: While specific 'family-friendly' designations aren't always explicit, many of the shorter, easier routes would be suitable for families. The varied terrain means you can choose paths that match your family's comfort and ability levels. Always check the distance and elevation gain before heading out.

Q: Is La Parra a good destination for dog-friendly hiking?
A: The region generally welcomes outdoor activities. While specific dog-friendly trails are not universally marked, many natural trails in the area are suitable for dogs, especially if kept on a leash. Always ensure your dog is well-behaved and that you clean up after them.

Q: What are some notable natural features or viewpoints in the area?
A: Beyond the limestone mountain range, the region offers impressive natural scenery. Nearby attractions like the Cuevas del Águila (Caves of the Eagle) showcase remarkable geological formations. You can also find viewpoints such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1574745" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of the Sierra de Tierra de Barros&lt;/a&gt;, offering panoramic vistas.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in La Parra?
A: Extremadura generally experiences hot summers, so spring and autumn are often considered the most pleasant seasons for hiking in La Parra, with milder temperatures. Winter can also be suitable, though some trails might be wetter. Always check local weather forecasts before your trip.

Q: Is there parking available near the trailheads in La Parra?
A: While specific parking information for every trailhead isn't detailed, La Parra is a rural municipality, and you can typically find parking in or near the village centers or designated areas close to popular trail starting points. It's advisable to check local maps or community information for specific parking recommendations for your chosen route.
tips: A beautiful place with wonderful panoramic views of the interior landscape of Badajoz. A detour to the mountains from La Parra will allow you to appreciate this beautiful landscape, highly recommended to do it when the sun goes down.

waypoints.name: View of the Dehesa landscape near La Parra
